prompt:
Our fulfilment service routes orders using a switch task from a workflow DSL document (see the rules in the tests: an expedite rule for rush orders, a bulk rule for orders of 100 or more, and a catch-all standard route). The DSL's contract is dead simple: cases are checked top to bottom, the FIRST case whose when condition holds routes the item, and the case without a when is the default, used only when nothing else matched.

Since the bulk rule went in — the first time we've had two conditions that can both be true for one order — routing has gone sideways in three ways: a rush order over the bulk threshold comes back routed to the standard queue, of all places; the warehouse log shows the expedite handler AND the bulk handler AND the standard handler all firing for that single order; and the catch-all fires even for orders that plainly matched a real rule. On top of that, dispatching an order that matches the first rule sometimes errors out complaining about a field the LATER rule's condition mentions — a condition that should never even have been looked at for that order.

We unit-tested the condition evaluation every which way and it is not the comparisons — something about how the engine walks the cases does not honor the DSL contract. Please track it down and fix it. go test -race ./... carries the failing reproductions; router_test.go, go.mod, and go.sum stay exactly as they are. Dependencies are pinned in the module files — go mod download fetches them.
